summary: Spanish architect and engineer (b. 1951, Valencia) known for curvilinear structures, cantilever-spar cable-stayed bridges, and organic forms. Major works: Turning Torso (Malmo), Milwaukee Art Museum Quadracci Pavilion (Burke Brise Soleil), World Trade Center Transportation Hub / Oculus (NYC), City of Arts and Sciences (Valencia). Also known for bridges including the Puente del Alamillo (Seville) and Zubizuri (Bilbao).

works.name: Turning Torso (Malmo, Sweden)
works.description: Skyscraper in Malmo, Sweden (tallest building in Sweden at completion; world's second twisting skyscraper). Constructed of nine twisted pentagonal segments; the top floor is rotated 90 degrees from the base. Based on Calatrava's own marble sculpture of a twisting human form. Adjacent to the Oresund Strait separating Malmo from Copenhagen.

clues.clue: Turning Torso: Sweden's tallest building; world's second twisting skyscraper; nine twisted pentagonal segments (top rotated 90 degrees from base); based on Calatrava's marble sculpture of a twisting human form
examples: His own marble sculpture of a twisting human form was the basis for a skyscraper by him constructed of twisted pentagonal segments in Malmo, Sweden
a tower that resembles a twisted spinal column, in this country's city of Malmo
the Turning Torso, a tower... whose top part is rotated 90 degrees from its base

works.name: Quadracci Pavilion, Milwaukee Art Museum
works.description: White ship-like addition to the Milwaukee Art Museum (which is housed in an Eero Saarinen War Memorial). The Quadracci Pavilion features Windhover Hall, whose chancel is shaped like the prow of a ship. The Burke Brise Soleil — a movable brise soleil of 72 white steel fin-like wings — opens and closes like a bird's wings. Part of an art complex also including the Kahler Building and Eero Saarinen's War Memorial Center. A large mast is used for the bridge connecting it.

clues.clue: Quadracci Pavilion, Milwaukee Art Museum: ship-like; Burke Brise Soleil — 72 movable white steel wings that open and close like a bird; chancel of Windhover Hall shaped like prow of a ship
examples: One of his buildings contains wing-like sunscreens called the Burke Brise Soleil, and is part of a building whose chancel is shaped like the prow of a ship
Moveable wings shade a pavilion designed for one of these buildings by Santiago Calatrava in Milwaukee
A white, ship-like building of this type in Milwaukee was designed by Santiago Calatrava

works.name: World Trade Center Transportation Hub (Oculus), New York City
works.description: Transportation hub at the World Trade Center site in New York City. The Oculus features white steel ribs emerging from the building, forming a pair of wings meant to evoke a bird released from a child's hands. Criticized for being poorly functional. Also designed the nearby St. Nicholas Greek Orthodox Church at the WTC site.

clues.clue: Oculus at WTC: white steel ribs forming wings evoking a bird released from a child's hands; criticized for being poorly functional
examples: White steel ribs emerging from another building in this complex form a pair of wings meant to evoke a bird released from a child's hands
An oval-shaped 'oculus' designed to resemble a bird leaving the hands of a child was built for a transportation hub designed by Santiago Calatrava
This creator of many cantilever bridges was criticized for his poorly-functioning World Trade Center transportation hub

works.name: City of Arts and Sciences (Valencia)
works.description: Cultural complex in Valencia, Spain, designed by Calatrava and Felix Candela. Built at the end of a sunken park in the former bed of the Turia River. Contains an IMAX theater whose outer concrete 'eyelid' can open to reveal a pool, an opera house named for Queen Sofia (sued for tile mosaics blown off — nicknamed 'white elephant'), and curvy aluminum brise soleils shading the buildings.

clues.clue: City of Arts and Sciences, Valencia: designed with Felix Candela; IMAX theater with outer concrete 'eyelid' opening to reveal pool; opera house named for Queen Sofia sued after tile mosaics blown off ('white elephant')
examples: The outer 'eyelid' of concrete can open to reveal a pool in an IMAX theater designed by this architect in the City of Arts and Sciences
After tile mosaics on one of these buildings nicknamed the 'white elephant' were blown off, its designer Santiago Calatrava was sued by the city of Valencia
This city commissioned Santiago Calatrava and Felix Candela to plan and build its cultural district, the City of Arts and Sciences

works.name: Bridges
works.description: Calatrava is celebrated for cantilever-spar cable-stayed bridges using a single inclined pylon or mast. Puente del Alamillo (Seville): harp-like, built for 1992 World's Fair on Isla de la Cartuja. Zubizuri (Bilbao): white tied-arch footbridge. Chords Bridge / Bridge of Strings (Jerusalem). Sundial Bridge, Turtle Bay (Redding, California). James Joyce Bridge and Samuel Beckett Bridge (Dublin, both over the Liffey; the Samuel Beckett Bridge resembles a harp on its side with 31 cables). Constitution Bridge (Venice, crossing the Grand Canal; criticized for being out of place; retrofitted with spherical wheelchair lift that overheated). Also: a bridge-like structure (large mast) for the Milwaukee Art Museum.

clues.clue: Known for cantilever-spar cable-stayed bridges; Puente del Alamillo (Seville, harp-like, 1992 World's Fair); Zubizuri (Bilbao, white tied-arch); Chords Bridge (Jerusalem); Sundial Bridge (Turtle Bay, California)
examples: name this Spanish architect known for cantilever spar cable-stayed bridges such as the Puente del Alamillo in Seville and the Chords Bridge in Jerusalem
His more famous structures include the harp-like Puente del Alamillo in Seville
Calatrava built the strikingly asymmetric Alamillo Bridge to provide access to the Isla de la Cartuja for a World's Fair in this city

clues.clue: Samuel Beckett Bridge (Dublin): resembles a harp laid on its side; 31 cables representing strings; rotates 90 degrees to allow boats through the Liffey
examples: It resembles a harp laid on its side, its 31 cables representing the instrument's strings
This bridge was built to rotate 90 degrees so larger boats could travel down the River Liffey

clues.clue: Constitution Bridge (Venice, Grand Canal): criticized for being out of place; retrofitted with spherical wheelchair lift that overheated and was scrapped
examples: A bridge in this city was retrofitted with a spherical wheelchair lift to alleviate concerns of inaccessibility, which was later scrapped on account of being too slow and overheating

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Santiago Calatrava Valls (b. 1951, Valencia, Spain). Spanish architect and structural engineer known for curvilinear, organic forms and bridges. Designed Bilbao Airport's main terminal; Stadelhofen Station (underground arcade in Zurich, one of his early works); Gare de Lyon Saint-Exupery in Lyon (bird-in-flight form); Gare do Oriente in Lisbon ('forest' of steel trees above); Athens Olympic Velodrome and stadium with retractable glass roof (2004); communications tower on Montjuic (Barcelona, 1992 Olympics, doubles as sundial); Tenerife Auditorio (massive wave-like cantilevered arc). Born same country as Antoni Gaudi. Designed a swimming pool hanging from the cupola of the ETH Zurich library.

clues.clue: Spanish architect known for curvilinear organic structures and cantilever bridges; born in Valencia (same country as Gaudi)
examples: name this Spanish architect known for cantilever spar cable-stayed bridges
Name this Spanish architect known for his curving structures
This controversial architect, known for his cantilever-spar cable-stayed bridges

clues.clue: Gare de Lyon Saint-Exupery (Lyon): used form of a bird in flight; Gare do Oriente (Lisbon): 'forest' of steel trees above; Stadelhofen (Zurich): underground arcade
examples: This architect used the form of a bird in flight for his new designs for the Gare de Lyon Saint-Exupery
This Spanish architect built an underground arcade beneath the Zurich Stadelhofen and a 'forest' of steel trees above Lisbon's Gare do Oriente

clues.clue: Communications tower on Montjuic (Barcelona 1992 Olympics) doubles as a sundial; Athens 2004 Olympic Velodrome and stadium with retractable glass roof
examples: A communications tower built by Calatrava on the Montjuic hill for this event doubles as a sundial

comprehensive_summary:
Santiago Calatrava Valls (b. 1951, Valencia, Spain) is a Spanish architect and structural engineer celebrated for his curvilinear, organic forms and cantilever-spar cable-stayed bridges. He was born in the same country as Antoni Gaudi.

Calatrava's most recognizable skyscraper is the Turning Torso in Malmo, Sweden — the country's tallest building and the world's second twisting skyscraper — which is constructed of nine twisted pentagonal segments such that the top floor is rotated 90 degrees from the base. It was based on Calatrava's own marble sculpture of a twisting human form. At the Milwaukee Art Museum, the Quadracci Pavilion takes the form of a ship: Windhover Hall's chancel is shaped like the prow of a ship, and the Burke Brise Soleil — 72 movable white steel fin-like wings — open and close like a bird. The pavilion is part of a complex also including the Kahler Building and Eero Saarinen's War Memorial Center.

At the World Trade Center site in New York, Calatrava designed the Transportation Hub (the 'Oculus'), whose white steel ribs form a pair of wings meant to evoke a bird released from a child's hands. He also designed the St. Nicholas Greek Orthodox Church at the same site. The Oculus was criticized for its poor functionality. For his hometown of Valencia, Calatrava constructed the City of Arts and Sciences with Felix Candela, sitting at the end of a sunken park in the former bed of the Turia River. The complex includes an IMAX theater whose outer concrete 'eyelid' can open to reveal a pool, and an opera house named for Queen Sofia, which Calatrava was later sued over after tile mosaics were blown off (the opera house was nicknamed 'the white elephant'). Curvy aluminum brise soleils shade many of the buildings.

Calatrava is celebrated for his bridges. The Puente del Alamillo in Seville (built for the 1992 World's Fair on the Isla de la Cartuja) is harp-like and asymmetric. The Zubizuri in Bilbao is a white tied-arch footbridge. The Chords Bridge (Bridge of Strings) is in Jerusalem, and the Sundial Bridge at Turtle Bay is in Redding, California. The Samuel Beckett Bridge in Dublin over the Liffey resembles a harp laid on its side, with 31 cables representing the strings; it rotates 90 degrees to let boats pass. The Constitution Bridge across Venice's Grand Canal was criticized for being out of place and was retrofitted with a spherical wheelchair lift that overheated and was later scrapped.

Among Calatrava's other notable works: the Stadelhofen Station (underground arcade, Zurich, an early work), the Gare de Lyon Saint-Exupery in Lyon (bird-in-flight form), the Gare do Oriente in Lisbon (a 'forest' of steel trees), a communications tower on Montjuic for the 1992 Barcelona Olympics that doubles as a sundial, an Olympic Velodrome and a stadium with a retractable glass roof for the 2004 Athens Olympics, the Tenerife Auditorio (massive wave-like cantilevered arc), and the Bilbao Airport terminal.
recursive_suggestions: City of Arts and Sciences Valencia
